title: wineventhook crate integration + SetWinEventHook focus-audit subsystem
goal: Per spec §6.6.4 (AC-QUIET-FOCUS-AUDIT) wire the `wineventhook` Rust crate to register a `SetWinEventHook(EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND, ..., WINEVENT_OUTOFCONTEXT | WINEVENT_SKIPOWNPROCESS)` global hook that logs every foreground transition `(timestamp, hwnd, pid, exe_name)` keyed by run_id. Harness assertion: no foreground change resolves to Handshake's pid or to any process spawned by Handshake per ProcessOwnershipLedger.
expected_diff_shape: Cargo.toml addition of wineventhook crate (Windows-only target); new module src/backend/handshake_core/src/operator_foreground/focus_audit.rs; FocusAuditLedger struct that writes to a per-run JSONL; tests assert the hook fires on real foreground changes during a synthetic test window.
unblock_conditions: MT-014 quiet window foundation; MT-007 ProcessOwnershipLedger so focus audit can correlate foreground PIDs against Handshake-spawned processes.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.6.4. Cargo.toml: add `[target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies] wineventhook = "<latest>"`. focus_audit.rs: `FocusAudit::start(run_id: RunId) -> FocusAuditHandle` registers the hook via wineventhook's `WinEventHook::new(EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND, EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND, WINEVENT_OUTOFCONTEXT | WINEVENT_SKIPOWNPROCESS, callback)`. The callback receives the HWND, resolves PID via `GetWindowThreadProcessId`, resolves exe_name via `QueryFullProcessImageNameW`, and appends a row `{ timestamp_utc, hwnd, pid, exe_name, run_id }` to a per-run JSONL file under `../gov_runtime/focus_audit/<run_id>.jsonl`. FocusAuditHandle has a `.stop() -> FocusAuditReport` method that unregisters the hook and returns the in-memory event list. FocusAuditReport: `{ run_id, total_events: usize, handshake_owned_events: Vec<FocusEvent>, foreign_events: Vec<FocusEvent> }`. The `handshake_owned_events` filter: PID matches current Handshake process OR PID exists in ProcessOwnershipLedger with parent_session_id matching any active session for this run_id. The harness assertion `assert_no_handshake_foreground(report)` panics if `report.handshake_owned_events.is_empty() == false`. Note: integration test difficulty — registering a real Win32 hook in a cargo test requires the test to run on a Windows host with a desktop session; for headless CI, gate with `#[cfg_attr(not(target_os = "windows"), ignore)]` and document that the test must run on Windows-with-desktop. Unit tests mock the wineventhook crate via a feature flag. Per HBR-QUIET-001 evidence_kind=headless_run_with_focus_audit this is exactly the audit subsystem that supplies the evidence.
risk_if_missed: Without focus audit, HBR-QUIET-001 'zero focus/Z-order change events during the test window' has no measurement; the quiet-mode invariant is unprovable.
validator_focus: Hook registration unregisters cleanly on Handle drop (no resource leak); per-run JSONL is human-readable and append-only; PID correlation with ProcessOwnershipLedger is queried via a typed API not raw SQL.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Handle drop unregisters the hook (RAII + explicit test).
PID correlation uses a typed ProcessOwnershipLedger query, not raw SQL embedded in focus_audit.
Test that requires Windows desktop is clearly gated and documented.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: wineventhook 0.11.0 wired to SYSTEM_FOREGROUND with skip_own_process; per-run JSONL ledger under runtime_root/gov_runtime/focus_audit/; PID correlation via typed OwnedProcessPidSet built from ReclaimableProcess.os_pid (no raw SQL); handle drop unhooks via RAII and explicit .stop() awaits the task.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Focus audit subsystem is entirely absent: no focus_audit.rs, no FocusAuditLedger, no wineventhook dependency, no per-run JSONL; without it HBR-QUIET-001 'zero handshake-owned foreground transitions' is unmeasurable.
